opps.title: NIH NICHD OIT Application and Website Support Services
opps.description:
The NICHD Office of Information Technology  requires continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance, Section 508 Compliance, database and data mart support, application infrastructure support, and other technical services including but not limited to the support of new and emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine learning (ML)/Robotic Process Automation (RPA), NICHD and/or NIH Wide. The focus of these IT support activities is creation, development, maintenance, operation, and support of a web application infrastructure that provides an efficient, effective, reliable, and secure computing environment in support of NICHD's and NIH's mission and supports staff in the efficient and effective utilization of these capabilities. 

Details on the evaluation criteria are contained in the attached RFQ.

In order to be considered for award, a vendor will be required to cover the SIN(s) listed below awarded on their Schedule contract. Any quote received from a contractor that does not cover the required SIN(s) will not be further evaluated and subsequently ineligible for award. Required SIN(s): 	54151S (Information Technology Professional Services); 541611 (Management and Financial Consulting, Acquisition and Grants Management Support, and Business Program and Project Management Services)
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institutes of Health (NIH), specifically the Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD), is seeking continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance. The goal of this contract is to ensure Section 508 Compliance, provide database and data mart support, and offer application infrastructure support, including the integration of new technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML).

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch
opps.summary:

opps.title: Communication Support
opps.description:
Amendment 00001

The purpose of this amendment is to cancel RFQ 15F06726Q0000273. Due to the overwhelming response to the RFQ, the Government realized that additional time is needed to clearly define its requirements. At this time, we do not have a date when this requirement will be resolicited; however, we encourage all interested vendors to continue to search SAM.gov for any future requirements. This statement by means obligates the Government to reissue a solicitation.

We thank all vendors for their interest in this requirement.

1 Scope
The purpose of this Statement of Work (SOW) is to procure professional video production, content creation, and communication support services for the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Office of Public Affairs (OPA). The services provided by the Contractor must enhance the FBI’s social media communication strategies, increase public awareness of FBI operational successes, and provide the public with actionable information to help prevent crime and fraud. The Contractor must provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and other items necessary to perform video production and communication services as described in this SOW.
This is a non-personal services contract. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over contractor employees performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.
1.1 Description of Services/Introduction
The FBI’s OPA requires video production and communication services to support FBI efforts in ensuring the effectiveness of existing social media communication strategies and content creation. Services will include the use of modern communication methods to increase the public’s awareness of key FBI operational successes and provide actionable information the public can use to protect themselves from becoming a victim of crime and fraud.
The Contrac

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

The requirements will encompass such functions as data entry, data loading, document processing, document indexing, document scanning, data validation, data quality assurance, data archiving, and overseeing projects.  

See attached RFQ 140E0126Q0028.

opps.title: Program Evaluation and Performance Management Services for the Sector Risk Management Agency (SRMA)
opps.description:
***This requirement is not planned for FY26.  However, there may be a follow-on in early FY27; record will be updated to reflect those changes.  Republishing for vendor awareness.***

The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) is requiring Program Evaluation and Performance Management Services for the Sector Risk Management Agency (SRMA).   The SRMA Program requires identification and guidelines for assessing the criticality and efficiency/efficacy of the SRMAs and responding to reduce risk to critical infrastructure by coordination through State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments, Federal Agencies and industry partners. The approaches for completion are various but jointly developed within and among the critical infrastructure sectors and SRMAs. The execution of the Sector Management role depends on the specific sector. It is influenced by external factors such as sector composition and profile, sector landscape, risk management maturity, and regulatory framework. The Sector Management role is also influenced by internal factors such as the type and extent of programs executed by other CISA divisions and CISA Regional Offices that may be more focused on some sectors than others or may have direct or quantifiable benefits in support of the sector. Work will be performed at a combination of the Contractorâ€™s facilities and the Department of Homeland Security at 4200 Wilson Boulevard, Arlington, Virginia.

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), through its Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization, aims to provide enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities. This initiative is designed to enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems, reducing duplication and lowering costs while accelerating modernization efforts.

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

opps.title: Emergency Communications Division (ECD) Modernization and Operations and Maintenance
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to modernize a legacy platform delivering priority telecommunications services and to provide operations and maintenance support for applications supporting the infrastructure protection and risk analysis mission spaces. These systems support CISAâ€™s mission to understand, manage, and reduce risk to the nationâ€™s critical cyber and physical infrastructure. CISA infrastructure analysis informs decisions made by DHS and public and private sector partners and assists CISA in framing policy and programs, prioritizing its operational activities and maximizing operational effectiveness through more efficient use of resources.

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, modernization of existing platforms to leverage Software as a Service (SaaS) and cloud native services; cloud-hosted mission system administration; information assurance management; certification and accreditation support; software patches and upgrades; configuration management; user management and support; backup and recovery; and performance monitoring.  This requirement does not include enterprise commodity IT operations, enterprise desktop support, enterprise network operations, or generalized agency-wide IT service desk support. 

This action will be competed and awarded against the multiple award Digital Transformation Support Services (DTSS) Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA)

Title: Database Services and Data Analysis Support for the United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program

Notice Type: Sources Sought / Request for Information (RFI)
Target GSA Schedule & SIN: Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) SIN 54151S (Information Technology Professional Services)
Anticipated NAICS Codes: 541512 (Computer Systems Design Services) or 541511 (Custom Computer Programming Services)

1.0 INTRODUCTION & BACKGROUND
This is a Request for Information (RFI) and Sources Sought notice issued solely for information and planning purposes; it does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. In accordance with 15.201(e), responses to this RFI are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  This notice is issued solely for market research and planning purposes, and the Government will not obligate any funds or reimburse respondents for any costs associated with preparing their submissions.

The United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program requires robust, secure, and highly analytical database services to support the tracking, management, and mitigation of corrosion across the enterprise. The CPAC program is critical to extending the lifecycle, ensuring the safety, and maintaining the combat readiness of USMC Ground Equipment.

The Government is seeking to identify qualified sources capable of providing specialized database management and analytical services, specifically those with demonstrated past performance working with USMC corrosion data and ground equipment platforms.

The primary objective of this RFI is to gauge industry capacity and capability to deliver comprehensive database services that support the USMC CPAC mission. The required services encompass data ingestion, secure storage, data architecture optimization, and a

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source Peace Corps' Marketing Campaign 2026-2029
opps.agency: Peace Corps Office of Acquisition and Contract Management
opps.description:
THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE, NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTES (RFQ). 

The United States Peace Corps, Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Acquisition and Contract Management (OCFO/ACM), intends to award a sole source, logical follow-on Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) under FAR 8.405-6(a)(1)(i)(C) for Marketing, Communications, Website, and Advertising Production Services.

This requirement supports the Peace Corps’ national-level Integrated Marketing Campaign and is essential to maintaining continuity of creative development, production, and deployment of mission critical outreach activities and volunteer recruitment. The planned anticipated BPA includes a base year and two one-year option periods, with an estimated total value of $30 million. All work shall be performed in strict accordance with the current Performance Work Statement and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of the GSA Schedule contract. 

Under the current BPA, the incumbent contractor is developing the approved 2027 national campaign. If the agency were to compete the follow-on BPA, any new contractor would be required to undergo 6–12 months of onboarding and discovery in advance of campaign development, resulting in unacceptable delays, substantial duplication of cost, and significant disruption to national outreach and recruitment activities. The Peace Corps is pursuing the strategic goal of placing 8,000 Volunteers by 2030. Continuity of national marketing operations is essential to achieving this objective. 

NO RFQ WILL BE ISSUED AND QUOTES WILL NOT BE REQUESTED. Capability statements will not be accepted.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Strong comms/outreach scope match — BLN24 proven prime (CMS MNPS $16M, CDC health comms, HUD FHEO outreach)
Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage
matched_lanes: Strategic Comms (CMS $16M, CDC $3.9M, HUD $2M — proven prime)

This will ensure enough time to respond before the solicitation period ends.  Please include the solicitation name and number as the subject line of the email.

opps.title: Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC)
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. This solicitation seeks to conduct a comprehensive revie

This is a notice of intent to issue a modification to extend the performance period of task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) issued against Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) 70RSAT22A00000001 with Silosmashers, Inc., 12001 Sunrise Valley Drive, Suite 202, Reston, VA 20191.  The task order will be modified to extend performance for 10 months on a non-competitive basis. The statutory authority permitting other than full and open competition is 41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1) implemented by the FAR Subpart 6.103-1 (FAR Class Deviation 25-11) entitled “Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements.”   

Background 

This modification to existing task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) will maintain support services to Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science & Technology Directorate (S&T) Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) in the areas of program management, business management, enterprise architecture alignment, IT governance, and regulatory compliance for platforms such as SharePoint, ServiceNow, and Microsoft Power Platform.  The task order includes labor, travel, and materials necessary to assist DHS S&T OCIO fulfill its mission.  The task order has a period of performance that began on September 30, 2025, and will expire on July 31, 2026, unless extended. The market research for the re-compete of 70RSAT25FC0000007 is under development. Due to the lapse in DHS appropriations in FY26, preparations for the re-compete have been significantly delayed.

Silosmashers is the current contractor for the requirement, and they possess unique and specialized knowledge of DHS S&T CIO requirements, activities, and stakeholders to ensure seamless, continuous support during the acquisition activities required for the re-compete. opps.title: Video Productions and Audiovisual Support for Projects - Joint Task Force-National Capitol Region
opps.description:
The Mission and Installation Contracting Command (MICC) Center – Fort Knox has a requirement from Production Acquisition Division (PAD), Joint Task Force-National Capital Region (JTF-NCR)/Military District Washington (MDW), J/G-42 Production Acquisition Division (PAD) to provide timely quality Video Productions and Audiovisual Support for Projects. The projects will support requirements generated by Army units, as well as other DoW and federal government agencies.

The vast majority of video productions are created to address specific training, information and/or communication objectives as identified by the requiring organization. Products are intended for different audiences ranging from senior DoW leadership and Congressional staff, to military servicemembers and their families, non-governmental organizations, community groups and the general public. Details of specific objectives and target audiences will be contained within the PWS of each individual task order isued under the Multi-Award Task Order Contracts (MATOC).

This RFP seeks to establish the initial pool of unpriced MATOCs on the Indefinate Delivery Indefnite Quantity (IDIQ) basis, with an ordering period of five (5) years. 

The Request for Proposal (RFP) and all attachments are attached and published with this notice.

*****************************************************************************************

Notice is amended to provide Government responses to Industry submitted questions and revised documents resulting from those answers.

The following documents have been attached/replaced:

opps.description:
Non-personal services to provide network sustainment and deployment support services to the Military Health System (MHS) Medical Treatment Facilities (MTFs) both inside and outside the continental United States (CONUS and OCONUS). This work includes functions such as network performance
measurement and monitoring, assistance with network design and development, network measurement, customer service in the form of trouble tracking and troubleshooting at the Tier 0 level, onsite support for both contractor and Government support teams for escalated trouble tickets, as well as maintenance and sparing support. Such services will be provided in accordance with stated MHS and specific Medical Component priorities. Services shall also comply with existing and evolving technical architecture guidance from international, commercial, DoD, and Health Affairs (HA) sources.  These services will be provided in a centralized and decentralized mode. The on-site network specialists will be the primary 24/7 on-call decentralized points of contact within the MTFs. Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

1. GENERAL INFORMATION:

This is a Sources Sought notice for market research purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation, a Request for Quote (RFQ), or a Request for Proposal (RFP), nor does it commit the Government to issue a solicitation in the future. This notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes to identify potential qualified sources. The Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this notice.


The purpose of this notice is to gain knowledge of potential qualified sources capable of providing non-personal services to deliver 46 Full-Time Equivalents (FTEs) across eight divisions within the USARCENT G3 Directorate and Command Group. Supported capabilities include modeling and simulation (M&S) based exercises, operational planning, current operations (OPCEN), target development, air and missile defense (AMD) synchronization, and strategic planning support. This requirement supports the USARCENT G3/5/7 Enterprise at Shaw AFB, SC (CONUS) and occasional forward travel to locations including Kuwait, Jordan, and the Udairi Range Complex (OCONUS).


The Government is conducting market research to determine if this requirement can be set aside for 8(a) Small Business participants. Responses from all business sizes are welcome. The Government's preference is to set this requirement aside for 8(a) participants if two or more capable, responsible 8(a) firms are identified at a fair market price (FAR 19.108-7).

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*

opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) in support of the National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA). This RFI is issued solely for market research and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP), solicitation, or commitment by DCSA to issue an RFP or to award a contract. The National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA) requires a comprehensive technical assessment and engineering design effort across its instructional spaces. The core requirement is to assess the specific hardware currently installed, evaluate the physical space constraints, and verify the exact types and capabilities of the existing A/V equipment. This engineering evaluation is necessary to address system performance gaps within NCCA's current footprint, specifically regarding audio quality in 36 training labs and centralized remote viewing capabilities for 18 isolated "pods" and 2 main classrooms.

See attached RFI for details.

opps.description:
GSA Wants Your Feedback to Enhance Domestic Preference in GSA Acquisition Programs - Market Research

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY

(NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the link into a new browser.)

Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.

NASA/NSSC has a requirement for ALTIUM ESSENTIALS | 3-DAY ONLINE TRAINING. 

NASA/NSSC intends to issue a sole source contract to ALTIUM LLC: 4225 Executive SQ STE 800; La Jolla, CA 92037 under the authority of FAR 12.102(a). It has been determined that ALTIUM LLC is the sole provider of ALTIUM ESSENTIALS | 3-DAY ONLINE TRAINING.

Oral communications are not acceptable in response to this notice.

Cody Guidry

opps.title: DISA Legacy Systems Application Management
opps.description:
See attached Sources Sought Notice and draft Performance Work Statement for full details.

The Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) is seeking sources to provide full life-cycle application management for their Legacy Systems.

This requirement is for full life-cycle application management of the Legacy System. The Legacy System primarily consists of Contract Support System (CSS), Contractual Online Procurement System (COPS), Financial Accounting and Budget System (FABS), and the Defense Information Technology Contracting Organization (DITCO) Web. This also includes other subsystems, such as: Rates and Tariffs File System (RTFS), Joint Hawaii Information Transfer System (JHITS), the Delivery Ticket Invoicing (DTI) process, Accounting & Audit Operations Office (CFA) Accounts Payable (A/P) Validation, Procurement Services (PS) A/P Validation, Information Technology (IT) Contract Closeout, Inquiry Quote Order (IQO) Contract Closeout, Telecom Voucher Management, and the European Voucher Module. 

Anticipated Period of Performance: June 10, 2027 – June 9, 2032

opps.title: Maintaining Momentum in More Than a Job States
opps.description:
This Request for Quotation (RFQ) is prepared in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8.4 and General Service Administration Regulation (GSAR) 538.71, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice.  Proposed pricing must be offered against the contractor's current schedule contract.

FNA is seeking a contractor to provide expert technical assistance to States to expand and improve their SNAP E&T programs. The period of performance is not to exceed a 12-month base period from the date of contract award. The Contractor shall accomplish the following over the period of performance:
-	Deliver program and policy related technical assistance to up to fifteen (15) States that have previously engaged or are planning to engage with FNA to develop a More Than a Job Campaign. The purpose of the technical assistance is to strengthen State SNAP E&T programs, increase their effectiveness, and address policy and program challenges that hinder successful implementation of the More Than a Job campaign. 
-	Develop and disseminate the SNAP E&T Review, a periodic digest of SNAP E&T relevant events, announcements and other news relevant to SNAP E&T.

This is an RFI for a non-personal services contract requirement, under which the Contractor is an independent Contractor to provide Employment Coaches to support the Employment Support Program for the Florida National Guard J9. The purpose of the Florida National Guard Employment Support Program is to provide employment opportunities and career options for members of the National Guard and reserve components. The objective of this requirement is to provide Employment Coaches throughout the state of Florida, and to provide technical support to managers, supervisors, reserve component members and family members as to the benefits and entitlements available through the Employment Support Program and to ensure standardization and continuity of service. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over the contract service providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.

HUD is issuing this Sources Sought synopsis as market research to identify qualified organizations with the capability, capacity, and resources to provide Global Market Advisory and Investors Analytics Services. The goal of this requirement is to improve efficiency and expand resources for linking the United States housing market to global capital markets, ultimately providing low-cost financing for federal housing programs. The results of this research will assist the government in determining the optimal procurement method and potential socio-economic set-asides.

opps.title: Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC)
opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.

opps.title: Integrated Workforce and Readiness Platform (ARMOR)
opps.description:
An Integrated Workforce and Readiness Platform for the Defense Health Agency

NAICS Code(s): 541511, 541512, and 541519 

1.0 Disclaimer
THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY. This RFI is being issued solely for information and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. This request for information does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Further, the Defense Health Agency (DHA) is not at this time seeking proposals and will not accept unsolicited proposals. Respondents are advised that the U.S. Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this RFI; all costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested party’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future RFP, if any is issued.

2.0 Background
The Defense Health Agency (DHA) is responsible for managing a complex, global healthcare enterprise. A primary challenge is the fragmentation of critical workforce data across distinct, standalone platforms. Manpower systems track billet assignments, credentialing systems manage clinical privileging, and separate databases monitor medical clearances, operational skillsets (JKSAs), and other position specific training. Because these systems lack native interoperability, leadership must manually reconcile who is assigned with what they are authorized, medically cleared, and operationally ready to perform.

This architectural disconnect introduces unacceptable risk, severely restricting our capacity to dynamically assess or model the downstream effects of operational deployments on our garrison healthcare mission. The goal of this initiative is to acquire a comprehensive solution that natively links these critical domains, providing a single, integrated view to meet the dynamic demands of both garrison healthcare and the operational environment.

D

opps.title: Facility Inspection & Detainee Death Review Services
opps.description:
The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is an organizational element within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Office of Detention Oversight (ODO) is a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office (ISP). ODO conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities in which detainees are housed for more than seventy-two (72) hours to determine compliance with the ICE 2025 and 2019 National Detention Standards (NDS), the 2025 Non-Dedicated Intergovernmental Detention Standards (NDIDS), the 2011 Performance Based National Detention Standards (PBNDS), as applicable, and the 2020 Family Residential Standards (FRS).
The External Reviews and Analysis Unit (ERAU) is also a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office. ERAU is responsible for conducting administrative reviews of all deaths of individuals in ICE custody. The Detainee Death Review (DDR) is a focused, fact finding, objective review of the facts and circumstances surrounding a detainee's death. ERAU also conducts special and focused reviews, as requested by ICE leadership.
The Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ), on behalf of ICE/ERAU is seeking a vendor who shall provide support services related to inspections and detainee death reviews to determine facility compliance with the NDS, NDIDS, PBNDS, or FRS, and for supporting designated analytical activities within ODO and ERAU.  The vendor will provide expert operational, analytical and administrative support for ODO and ERAU inspections, reviews, and special assignments.
The vendor shall provide productivity and management support, including but not limited to, quality control; data input; clerical support; statistical and analytical documentation and data manipulation; training of personnel; and other related functions. The vendor's project managers for both inspections and DDRs shall perform day to day management of overall contract support operations and ensure conformity with all contract

opps.title: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Case Management System (CMS)
opps.description: The DHA Office of Equal Employment and Resolution Management (OEERM) office requires this automated tracking system to ensure accurate management and oversight of EEO Complaints for the Agency or the production of the annual 462 report in accordance with regulatory compliance. The goal of this requirement is to continue to maintain this commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) product (Case Management Development Platform powered by Entellitrak) in order to provide a system capable of ensuring the agency is within regulatory compliance. Similar products will be implemented for other capabilities such as Anti-Harassment (AH). This COTS solution will continue to be hosted on Military Cloud (milCloud) development, test and production environments. Additional responsibility for this effort will be to provide operations, sustainment and engineering support as well as system updates and maintenance of all non-production environments.

opps.title: OneABMC Microsoft Enterprise Support Services
opps.description:
American Battle Monuments Commission uses a Microsoft enterprise environment that includes Microsoft 365 collaboration and productivity services, identity and device management, security and monitoring services, Azure cloud services, Dynamics 365 Customer Service, and Power Platform business applications to support agency operations across many locations. ABMC requires reliable technical and project management support for collaboration tools, identity, device management, security, monitoring, records and compliance, and business applications. Under this requirement, Contractor shall help ABMC keep these services running, improve governance, and support continuous improvement.

This requirement includes an optional Firm-Fixed-Price transition CLIN and Time & Materials support and development CLINs. See the Performance Work Statement for complete CLIN structure and pricing requirements

Questions are due no later than 11:59 PM Eastern Time on Tuesday, July 14, 2026. ABMC reserves the right to respond to questions received after this date at its discretion. Proposals are due no later than 10:00 AM Eastern Time on Friday, July 24, 2026. Any extension of the proposal due date will be issued only if determined necessary by ABMC.

Solicitation number FA8201-26-R-0015 is issued as a request for proposal (RFP) for Industrial Wastewater Treatment Plant Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition System and Cybersecurity upgrade at Hill AFB UT. Please see attached doucment for details. 

Questions are to be submitted in writing to Brian Green at brian.green.20@us.af.mil and Bryce Michelson at bryce.michelson@us.af.mil, due no later than 17 July 2026 at 10:00am MDT

A site visit is scheduled for 14 July 2026 at 9:00am MDT. Offerors are strongly encouraged to attend the site visit to ensure that there is a complete understanding of the requirements stated in the attached SOW. An award will not be modified to accommodate an Offeror's failure to understand the requirement. Offerors wanting to attend will need to provide the following information: Names (Last, First & Middle), date of birth, driver's license number, and state of issuance. This information is due no later than 2:00pm MDT on 9 July 2026 to brian.green.20@us.af.mil and bryce.michelson@us.af.mil. No more than 3 employees per company will be permitted.



Posting History:

30 June 2026: Added Site Walk and updated dates

opps.title: NIJ-NamUs (National Missing and Unidentified Persons System)
opps.description:
NOTE 1: RFQ submission due date is MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET.
Submit all quotations by MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 at 12:00PM ET.
GSA eBuy system stated close date of Wednesday, July 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET is INCORRECT.
NOTE 2: ---RFQ1814958 (100% small business set-aside) HAS BEEN CANCELED-- 
RFQ1815768 (unrestricted) REPLACES RFQ 1814958--- Submit quotations to RFQ1815768 only.

National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ). The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to:
(1) operate without interruption,
(2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and
(3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.

The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.

Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor. The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them. Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---

Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: 89th Administrative Security Support Services
opps.description:
The contractor will be required to provide all personnel and expertise required to perform comprehensive administrative, personnel, and physical security support to the 89th Attack Squadron (89 ATKS) at Ellsworth Air Force Base, South Dakota. The selected Contractor will independently execute and manage all designated functional security areas to ensure complete operational coverage and regulatory compliance with applicable DoD, AF, and Intelligence Community Directives (ICDs). Ultimately, the objective is to maintain continuous, secure operations within the squadron's footprint by managing all collateral and SCI security programs required for assigned personnel to execute their combat and training missions.

**Critical Eligibility Notes: Vendors MUST possess an active, verifiable Top Secret Facility Clearance at the time of proposal submission. Additionally, proposed personnel MUST possess active, adjudicated TS/SCI clearances eligible for performance on Day 1.**
The Period of Performance: This effort includes a 1-year Base Period, (4) 1-year option periods, and a 6-month optional extension.


Questions are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 15 July 2026. Please reference the solicitation number and request title in the subject line of all email inquiries.
Proposals are to be sent to the Contract Administrator and OCO at the email addresses listed in this RFP not later than 12:00pm MST, 30 July 2026.

opps.title: Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) Support
opps.description:
The 763rd Enterprise Sourcing Squadron (763 ESS) is seeking sources to provide on-going support that provides Headquarters Air Mobility Command Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs (HQ AMC A5/8) with the workforce necessary to manage Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) operational capabilities and requirements derived from validated user needs.

The 763rd ESS is seeking information for potential sources to provide MAF EFB Support. Headquarters Air Mobility Command’s (HQ AMC) Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs’ (A5/8) primary responsibility is to oversee tanker and airlift concepts, budget strategies, manpower and organization, competitive sourcing, strategic planning and metrics, programs and policy, operations planning analysis, airlift and tanker doctrine, intergovernmental agreements, congressional interests, and management of the command’s validated acquisition programs.

The EFB is a critical component to aircrew operations. The EFB is an electronic handheld device that enables removal of carry-on paper technical orders/flight manuals and provides ancillary situational awareness to the operator. Over time, the EFB has become critical and necessary equipment for aircrew operations. Since inception, support contractors have been necessary to assist with requirements development and sustainment of the EFB. This PWS is intended to continue necessary contractor support. The contractor shall support HQ AMC/A5R in managing EFB technical requirements and capabilities.

opps.title: Modernization of Logistics and Finance Business Systems (PM LOG‑FIN formerly EBS‑C) – Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS)
opps.description:
This Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS), Notice ID LOGFIN26, is issued under the Army Open Solicitation (AOS) W9128Z‑25‑S‑A002 for the U.S. Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC‑RI). The CFS seeks innovative commercial Low‑Code/No‑Code (LCNC) and modernization solutions to support the Army Logistics and Finance communities through rapid, iterative prototyping aligned to nine Areas of Interest (AOI) representing PM LOG‑FIN Value Chains.

This CFS remains open in perpetuity until canceled. The Government will publish separate Targeted Calls for Solutions (TCFS) under this CFS, each with specific requirements and deadlines. Industry may also submit AOI White Papers at any time identifying capability gaps within the AOIs.

Submissions must address LCNC and modernization capabilities supporting non‑transactional and transactional functions integrating with five current Army ERP systems. Solutions affecting financial transactions must meet DoD auditability, internal controls (ICOFR/ITGC), traceability, and segregation of duties requirements. All solutions must comply with Army cybersecurity, governance, and interoperability standards and be deployable in cArmy 2.0.

The nine Areas of Interest (AOIs) include:
• AOI 01: Mission Operations Support
• AOI 02: Asset Acquisition
• AOI 03: Real Estate
• AOI 04: Supply
• AOI 05: Fulfillment
• AOI 06: Transportation
• AOI 07: Maintenance
• AOI 08: Manufacturing / Remanufacturing
• AOI 09: Resource Allocation

Approved LCNC platforms include Appian, Army Vantage, Palantir, PowerApps, Salesforce, SAP, and ServiceNow.
Submissions must follow all instructions in this announcement. Questions and submissions must be sent to usarmy.ria.acc.mbx.pmlogfin-cso@army.mil. 

The Army Open Solicitation (AOS) that this Call for Solution is associated with can be found at:

https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/09ab570a35484c6cb5c6eba05cad19b3/view

Please review the AOS posting an

opps.title: DCSA INNOVATION GATEWAY
opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) Innovation Gateway is a competitive solicitation that will result in the award of prototype projects under Other Transaction Agreements (OTA).

It is our commercially-focused front door for innovators.

Our goal is simple: to partner with creative companies to solve our most critical mission challenges.

Innovation Gateway Process

The Innovation Gateway is a general announcement of DCSA’s general areas of interest (Digital Transformation Enablement, Data Integration and Advanced Analytics, Cyber and Industrial Security, Digital Workforce and Governance). The general soliciation includes criteria for selecting Solution Concept Papers and soliciting the participation of all proposers capable of satisfying the Government’s needs.

Proposal requests will be issued via ‘Innovation Calls’ that are published separately under the DCSA Innovation Gateway at various times during the open period of this solicitation. 

Innovation Calls will be published via Sam.gov through separate solicitation postings referencing the DCSA Innovation Gateway. Proposers are required to use the Solution Concept Paper Template (DCSA Innovation Gateway Attachment 1) to submit their solutions.

The Government will only accept proposals in response to Innovation Calls. Unsolicited proposals that are not in response to an Innovation Call will not be reviewed by the Government.

opps.title: Access Required to Online Real Estate Database Services
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items/services prepared in accordance with the format in Federal Acquisition Regulations (FAR) subpart 12.6, “Streamlined Procedures for Evaluation and Solicitation for Commercial Items or Services”, and FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotes are being requested. This solicitation number is 2043FY-26-Q-00038 and is issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ). 

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is seeking commercial real estate database subscription services to support the Large Business and International (LB&I) Division's Engineering Program.

The LB&I Division’s Engineering Program provides technical support on engineering and valuation issues involving large and complex business tax returns. Specialists perform work involving commercial real estate appraisal, machinery and equipment valuation, cost segregation studies, and research credit claims.

To support these activities, the Government requires access to a commercial real estate information platform that provides nationwide current and historical market data. The platform must provide comprehensive commercial real estate information, including ownership records, sales transactions, lease information, market trends, and related market analytics necessary to evaluate commercial properties and support tax administration activities. The Government intends to award a contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation conforms to the solicitation requirements and offers the lowest evaluated price. This requirement is unrestricted for full and open competition.

The Statement of Work and all applicable solicitation documents are included as attachments to this notice. Sources who believe they can provide the products/services included in the Statement of Work (SOW) shall submit a price quote via email

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA. The NAVCC is responsible for acquiring, preserving, and providing access to the world’s largest collection of films, television and radio broadcasts, videos, and sound recordings. 

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Bloomberg Industry Group, Inc, 1801 South Bell Street Arlington, VA 22202.

 Background

In support of the S&T mission, many divisions need access to certain scientific journals and databases to remain at the forefront of their research.  Bloomberg Government is a premier platform for federal policy, budget, and legislative intelligence. The proposed purchase will enable DHS S&T staff to access real-time government data, track legislative developments, and conduct policy analysis, thereby enhancing operational effectiveness and strategic decision-making. The acquisition is expected to streamline research, improve situational awareness, and support mission-critical activities.  S&T will be procure five  (5) subscriptions for (5) users.

Bloomberg Government (BGOV) is a unique, proprietary platform that provides comprehensive, real-time federal legislative, regulatory, and budgetary intelligence specifically tailored for government affairs professionals. No other commercially available product offers the same breadth and depth of integrated data, advanced analytics, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards in a single solution. BGOV’s exclusive aggregation of congressional activity, appropriations tracking, policy analysis, and federal contract intelligence is essential for DHS S&T’s mission to monitor and respond to rapidly evolving legislative and regulatory developments. This is a notice of intent, not a request for a quotation. A solicitation document will not be issued and quotations will not be requested.

opps.title: Depot Operations Support
opps.description:
The United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA) Client Experience Center (CEC) is accountable for managing the Information Technology (IT) infrastructure and service delivery to USDA. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot's primary mission is to provide centralized and standardized device deployment and excessing services to over 100,000 USDA customers across the United States and its territories. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot is a hybrid facility that performs receiving, storage, inventory management, asset management, device (laptop, desktop, and tablet) preparation, OS imaging/deployment, kitting, pick and ship, returns processing, shipping, and excessing of IT equipment such as laptops, desktops, tablets, mobile printers, monitors, cords, keyboards, flash drives, cable locks, and mice. The USDA Enterprise Depot is partnered and integrated with 3rd party vendors to assist with overflow storage and shipping services.

opps.description:
Provide services in accordance with the attached Statement of Work. The primary goal of establishing this contract is for electronic security consulting services to support the procurement and design phases of the U.S. Marshals Service (USMS) electronic security system project for Courthouses in Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN.

Please read the attached documents for information on the scope of work and additional information included. The consulting services outlined focus on supporting GSA through scope development, design review, procurement, and initial security contractor onboarding for new electronic security systems (Access Control, Video Surveillance, Intrusion Detection, and Intercom/Emergency Communication). Services will primarily be performed remotely, with necessary on-site activities at Washington, D.C., Atlanta, GA, Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN. The estimated duration for these services is 2-years from the commencement date, with implementation support projected from August 3, 2026, to August 3, 2028.

The Government intends to award an order for services in accordance with FAR 8.4 (GSA Class Deviation RFO-2025-08) on the basis of best value to the Government. All evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are approximately equal to price.

The Offeror shall demonstrate past experience designing, installing, integrating, testing, and commissioning electronic security systems for facilities of similar size, scope, and complexity. The Indian Health Service, Northern Navajo Medical Center (NNMC), located in Shiprock, New Mexico intends to negotiate a sole source award with Hardenbergh Group Inc. The North American Industry Classification System or NAICS code is 541618 Other Management Consulting Services $19M.



The required services include independent physician peer review, Initial Professional Practice Evaluations (IPPE), Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluations (OPPE), and professional practice reviews for inpatient and outpatient medical records. The contractor shall provide board-certified physician reviewers who are independent, free from conflicts of interest, and qualified in the applicable specialty under review. Reviews may be required in multiple clinical disciplines, including but not limited to Emergency Medicine, Pediatrics, Internal Medicine, Rheumatology, Family Medicine, General Surgery, Obstetrics and Gynecology, Orthopedics, Podiatry and Wound Care, Optometry, Neurology, Pathology, Radiology, Rehabilitation Services, and Psychiatry.



The contractor must be capable of performing timely medical record reviews, including urgent reviews with completion generally required within twenty-one (21) days of receipt of records. The contractor shall facilitate direct communication between hospital leadership and designated physician reviewers, provide reviewer qualifications and curriculum vitae upon request, and maintain established review procedures. All reviews must assess whether the provider's care and documentation meet the applicable specialty standard of care.



The contractor shall comply with all applicable privacy and confidentiality requirements, including HIPAA Business Associate obligations, maintain secure handling of protected health information, and ensure proper disposition of records following completion of reviews. Title
Sources Sought / Request for Information   Insurance Entry and Verification Buffer Support Services

Notice
The Department of Veterans Affairs [VA], Regional Procurement Office - Central [RPOC], is conducting market research to identify capable sources for a potential requirement for Insurance Entry and Verification Buffer Support Services supporting Consolidated Patient Account Center [CPAC] Revenue Operations [RO] nationwide. This Sources Sought / Request for Information [RFI] is issued solely for information and planning purposes and does not constitute a solicitation, request for quote, or request for proposal. The Government is not obligated to issue a solicitation or award a contract as a result of this notice. Responses are being used to support market research, acquisition planning, evaluation of whether the requirement can be acquired as a commercial service, and potential set aside determination..

The draft requirement contemplates contractor provided nationwide insurance entry and verification services, including labor, supervision, management, equipment, and other resources necessary to perform insurance entry and verification of VA patient insurance information in authorized VA healthcare systems and related interfaces. Core functions include insurance entry and verification, eligibility verification, coordination of benefits, discrepancy resolution, rejected entry resolution, required reporting, and compliance with HIPAA, VA privacy, and VA information security requirements.

Performance is expected to align with draft standards such as 93 percent on-time completion of assigned entries, at least 95 percent data accuracy, 100 percent review and action on rejected entries within five [5] business days, required participation in Government directed coordination calls, maintenance of required systems and portal access, and no substantiated privacy or security violations. opps.title: Support Services to Conduct Risk Analysis in Support of the U.S. Coast Guard Office of Performance Management and Assessment (DCO-81) and Domestic Port Security Evaluation Division (CG-PSA-2
opps.description:
The purpose of this Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) is to quantitatively assess, measure and communicate risk in the maritime domain from all threats and hazards to support risk management efforts owned or initiated by the Deputy Commandant of Operations (DCO). This BPA combines all strategic operational risk analysis initiatives currently conducted for the DCO under a single framework supporting strategic planning and program management.

The BPA will primarily support two (2) initiatives in place to assess and model maritime risk: the Maritime Security Risk Analysis Model (MSRAM) and the Risk Based Maritime Security Response Operations (RBMSRO) optimization tool. Other operational risk assessments and enterprise risk management efforts are also expected to leverage this BPA.

Supporting the MSRAM includes determining security risk to Critical Infrastructure and Key Resources (CIKR) from terrorist attacks that could have significant national and international impacts. CIKR are assets, systems, and/or resources essential to the economy, public health, safety, and security of the nation. Examples of CIKR covered in this BPA are port infrastructure and systems, waterways, vessels and facilities, petroleum products, liquefied natural gas, nuclear material, cargo and tank vessels as well as other vessels, sites and structures designated as CIKR. Maritime CIKR (MCIKR) patrol is operationalized through the key port areas (KPA) patrol concept and the Risk Based Maritime Security Response Operations (RBMSRO) tool. Supporting the RBMSRO optimization tool includes effectively analyzing USCG PWCS missions accomplished by law enforcement assets; providing USCG with a means to plan, execute, and report Maritime Security Response Operations activities. In addition, this BPA will support the enhancement of risk communication using existing information technology in the USCG and Federal Government enterprise.

NATURE OF REQUIREMENT:

Request for Quote, Security Card Reader System Replacement

In accordance with Federal Supply Schedules Contracts, please submit a quote on the above-mentioned project in accordance with the attached documents. 

DELIVERY AND PERFORMANCE PERIOD REQUIREMENTS

Delivery Date:
The total period of performance for this task order is 30 days after notice to proceed. 

Place of Performance:
All work shall be performed at the following location:
Robert A. Young (RAY) Federal Building 
1222 Spruce Street 
St. Louis, MO 63103

PROPOSAL SUBMISSIONS

Proposals shall consist of the following documents, completed and executed in accordance with this Solicitation:

Price Proposal

The Price Proposal shall consist  a detailed cost breakout. The cost break down shall sum to the total cost of the price proposed for the project. The price proposal acceptance period shall be 90 days from the request for proposal due date.

Deadline for Submissions:

Provide a proposal on or before July 23, at 3:00 p.m. Central Standard Time.  

Submit proposals electronically to the Contracting Officer at: stephan.harris@gsa.gov or uploaded to the GSA eBbuy Website.

Electronic Submission of Proposals

Offers sent shall be deemed timely if received by the email address of the GSA Point of Contact (POC) listed herein on or before the date and time established for receipt of offers, as recorded by the Government computer system's documentation of the exact official time of receipt, not by the time it was sent by the Offeror or received on the GSA server. It is the Offeror's responsibility to ensure proposals are complete and submitted early enough to be received by the GSA POC before the deadline, and to confirm receipt before the deadline. Proposals or portions of proposals which are received by the GSA POC later than the time noted within will not be accepted, reviewed, or considered.

NOTE: The maximum file size is 25 MB, therefore contractors/offerors need to plan accordingly.

opps.title: Defense Energy Consortium (DECo) - Consortium Management Organization (CMO)
opps.description:
Please only submit a Proposal if you are true Consortium Management Organization (CMO). If you are just intersted in being a member of the consortium once it is established please send an email to the Mr. Brock Moon and Mr. Nicholas Kimmey that you are interested in becoming a member of the eventual consortium and we will pass your information along to the CMO. 

Department of Defense (DoD) installations largely rely on off-site energy providers to support land, air, sea, space, and cyberspace-based national security missions. This dependence creates unacceptable risks, because grid outages caused by maintenance issues, extreme weather, and attacks by determined adversaries may impact our national security missions. To ensure mission continuity even during a grid outage, congress has mandated that DoD achieve by 2030 at least 99.9% energy resilience year-round (i.e., no more than 8.76 hours of downtime annually) for all of its critical missions at all DoD installations (See 10 U.S.C. § 2920).

The Department of the Air Force (DAF) alone has identified nearly 4,000 resilience gaps at its installations worldwide that need rapid and cost-effective mitigation. Meeting this challenge requires DAF to access innovative energy resilience technologies, deploy commercial off-the-shelf technologies, and implement new business models at an enterprise-wide scale with commercial speed.



Concerned that DoD and the military services are unable to access or deploy these technologies and business models at the scale, speed, and cost-effectiveness required to meet its energy resilience challenges, Congress has encouraged DoD to leverage its authority pursuant to 10 U.S.C. § 4022, to partner with a third-party consortium dedicated to military installation resilience and energy innovation.[1]

10 U.S.C. § 4022 authorizes DoD to conduct prototype projects that are directly relevant to enhancing DoD’s mission effectiveness. Other Transaction Authority (OTA) is an al

This is a pre-solicitation notice of a proposed contract action issued in accordance with part 12 – Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This is not a solicitation or invitation for offerors to submit bids.

The website provides download instructions.  Any secure/locked documents (if applicable) uploaded with the solicitation will require an active Sam.gov vendor registration for access.  If you encounter problems accessing these documents, please contact the Federal Service Desk at 866-606-8220.  All future information about this solicitation, including any amendments, will also be distributed solely through this website.  Interested parties are responsible for continuously monitoring this website to ensure that they have the most recent information about this proposed contract action.

IMPORTANT: Any contractor interested in obtaining a contract award with the Federal Bureau of Prisons must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov. All offerors are urged to double check their SAM registration
